title: Podman server uses current working directory's Dockerfile instead of the one from input tarball
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Hi,

I have trouble when using the podman remote service when the process accepting connection has a Dockerfile in its current working directory.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I don't have a machine with podman directly available, so I install it inside a container started with docker (started with: docker run -t -i --rm --privileged fedora) then from this fedora image I install podman (dnf install -y podman)

From this empty fedora image, to reproduce my issue:

```
[root@cf8dc9c7094f /]# mkdir folder1
[root@cf8dc9c7094f /]# cd folder1/
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder1]# echo "FROM ubuntu" > Dockerfile
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder1]# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --storage-driver=vfs --events-backend=file system service -t 0 &
[1] 324
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder1]# mkdir /folder2
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder1]# cd /folder2
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# echo "FROM fedora" > Dockerfile
```

Then the first build wrongly builds the ubuntu image (from /folder1, where the podman server is running), while the podman client is in /folder2 where it should build a Fedora image:

```
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# podman --remote build .
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:00:12:39 +0000] "GET /v3.4.0/libpod/_ping HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:00:12:39 +0000] "GET /v3.4.0/libpod/_ping HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
STEP 1/1: FROM ubuntu
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:7b1a6ab2e44dbac178598dabe7cff59bd67233dba0b27e4fbd1f9d4b3c877a54
Copying blob sha256:7b1a6ab2e44dbac178598dabe7cff59bd67233dba0b27e4fbd1f9d4b3c877a54
Copying config sha256:ba6acccedd2923aee4c2acc6a23780b14ed4b8a5fa4e14e252a23b846df9b6c1
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
COMMIT
--> ba6acccedd2
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:00:12:39 +0000] "POST /v3.4.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%2C%7B%22Name%22%3A%22network%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&seccomp=%2Fusr%2Fshare%2Fcontainers%2Fseccomp.json&shmsize=67108864&t= HTTP/1.1" 200 779 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
ba6acccedd2923aee4c2acc6a23780b14ed4b8a5fa4e14e252a23b846df9b6c1
```


If I remove the Dockerfile from /folder1, then it correctly builds the fedora image:

```
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# mv /folder1/Dockerfile /folder1/Dockerfile.bak 
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# podman --remote build . 
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:00:14:21 +0000] "GET /v3.4.0/libpod/_ping HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:00:14:21 +0000] "GET /v3.4.0/libpod/_ping HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
STEP 1/1: FROM fedora
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:944c4b241113c7047b3aa00c286aee989dc5b808ec0cae27d79d8dba87501ece
Copying blob sha256:944c4b241113c7047b3aa00c286aee989dc5b808ec0cae27d79d8dba87501ece
Copying config sha256:191682d6725209667efcfd197c4dc93be5ab33729b7a4a2a45d5cf2bc1f589e0
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
COMMIT
--> 191682d6725
@ - - [21/Oct/2021:00:14:21 +0000] "POST /v3.4.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%2C%7B%22Name%22%3A%22network%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&seccomp=%2Fusr%2Fshare%2Fcontainers%2Fseccomp.json&shmsize=67108864&t= HTTP/1.1" 200 788 "" "Go-http-client/1.1"
191682d6725209667efcfd197c4dc93be5ab33729b7a4a2a45d5cf2bc1f589e0
```


It seems unexpected that for the podman server the Dockerfile found in the current working directory is used in preference of the dockerfile found in the input tarball.

Cheers,
Romain


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --storage-driver=vfs --events-backend=file version
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Thu Sep 30 19:40:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --storage-driver=vfs --events-backend=file info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.23.1
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpuset
  - cpu
  - cpuacct
  - memory
  - devices
  - freezer
  - net_cls

  - net_prio
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.30-2.fc34.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.30, commit: '
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: container
    version: "34"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: cf8dc9c7094f
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 3.10.0-1160.42.2.el7.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: k8s-file
  memFree: 3410477056
  memTotal: 16806350848
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-1.2-1.fc34.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.2
      commit: 4f6c8e0583c679bfee6a899c05ac6b916022561b
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false   
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.12-2.fc34.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.12
      commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3
      libslirp: 4.4.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.0
  swapFree: 843575296
  swapTotal: 1073737728
  uptime: 301h 42m 3.64s (Approximately 12.54 days)
plugins:
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - journald
  network:
  - bridge
  - macvlan
  volume:
  - local
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: vfs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus: {}
  imageStore:
    number: 2
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.4.0
  Built: 1633030821
  BuiltTime: Thu Sep 30 19:40:21 2021
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.16.8
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.4.0
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[root@cf8dc9c7094f folder2]# rpm -q podmanrpm -q podman
package podmanrpm is not installed
podman-3.4.0-1.fc34.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Tested inside a fedora image, itself started by a Docker daemon, with --privileged, on a RHEL 7 x86-64 kernel.

comments.body: Note: I did try using docker as a client, and exporting DOCKER_HOST=unix://run/podman/podman.sock, and the same issue happens. So the issue really is on the server side, not client side.
